DIA_JM_ANNUAL_CU.name: Annual Checkup
DIA_JM_ANNUAL_CU.description: An Annual Checkup is a yearly body checkup to see if there is any problems with their health or body. They are usually done by General Practitioners or Internal Medicine doctors.

DIA_JM_CARDIO_CU.name: Cardiovascular Checkup
DIA_JM_CARDIO_CU.description: An Cardiovascular Checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems within the circulatory system. They are usually performed by Cardiologists.

DIA_JM_NEURO_CU.name: Neurologic Checkup
DIA_JM_NEURO_CU.description: A Neurologic Checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems within the nervous system. They are usually performed by Neurologists.

DIA_JM_GASTRO_CU.name: Gastrointestinal Checkup
DIA_JM_GASTRO_CU.description: A Gastrointestinal Checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems within the digestive system. They are usually performed by Gastroenterologists.

DIA_JM_NEPHRO_CU.name: Nephrologic Checkup
DIA_JM_NEPHRO_CU.description: A Nephrologic checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems within the renal system. It is usually performed by Nephrologists.

DIA_JM_PNEUMO_CU.name: Respiratory Checkup
DIA_JM_PNEUMO_CU.description: A Respiratory Checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems within the repiratory system. They are usually performed by Pulmonologists or Internal Medicine Doctors.

DIA_JM_NUTRI_CU.name: Dietary Checkup
DIA_JM_NUTRI_CU.description: A Dietary Checkup is a checkup to see if there is any problems with their diet and vitamin levels. They are usually performed by Nutritionists.

DIA_JM_COVID_CU.name: Post-COVID Checkup
DIA_JM_COVID_CU.description: The patient has made a appointment with their General Practitioner after being infected with COVID-19. They may have Long COVID/Post-COVID Syndrome/Post-Viral Fatigue.

DIA_JM_VACCINE_FU.name: Post-Vaccine Followup
DIA_JM_VACCINE_FU.description: The patient has made a appointment with their General Practitioner after taking a COVID vaccine. They are having adverse effects and/or some minor symptoms.

DIA_JM_INFLU_VAC.name: Influenza Vaccination
DIA_JM_INFLU_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Influenza vaccine will be adinistered prevent H1N1, H3N1 and 2 different types of Influenza B infections.

DIA_JM_ANTHRAX_VAC.name: Anthrax Vaccination
DIA_JM_ANTHRAX_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Anthrax vaccine will be administered to prevent Anthrax.

DIA_JM_DENGUE_VAC.name: Dengue Fever Vaccination
DIA_JM_DENGUE_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Dengue Fever vaccine will be administered to prevent Dengue Fever.

DIA_JM_DTAP_VAC.name: DTaP Vaccination
DIA_JM_DTAP_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (DTaP) vaccine will be administered to prevent Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (Whooping Cough).

DIA_JM_EBOLA_VAC.name: RVSV-ZEBOV Vaccination
DIA_JM_EBOLA_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, the rVSV-ZEBOV vaccine will be administered to prevent Ebola.

DIA_JM_MALARIA_VAC.name: Malaria Vaccination
DIA_JM_MALARIA_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Malaria vaccine will be administered to prevent Malaria.

DIA_JM_MMR_VAC.name: MMR Vaccination
DIA_JM_MMR_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Mumps, Measles and Rubella (MMR) vaccine will be administered prevent Mumps, Measles and Rubella.

DIA_JM_TB_VAC.name: Tuberculosis Vaccination
DIA_JM_TB_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Tuberculosis vaccine will be administered to prevent Tuberculosis.

DIA_JM_VARICL_VAC.name: Varicella Vaccination
DIA_JM_VARICL_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Varicella vaccine will be administered to prevent Chickenpox and Shingles.

DIA_JM_AZCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - AstraZeneca
DIA_JM_AZCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Oxford-AstraZeneca), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_SINOCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - SinoVac
DIA_JM_SINOCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (SinoVac-CoVac), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_SINOPCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - Sinopharm
DIA_JM_SINOPCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Sinopharm), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_CANCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - CanSino
DIA_JM_CANCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (CanSino), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_PFICOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - Pfizer
DIA_JM_PFICOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Pfizer-BioNTech), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_MODCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - Moderna
DIA_JM_MODCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Moderna), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_JNJCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - Johnson N Johnson
DIA_JM_JNJCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Johnson n Johnson), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_SPUTCOV_VAC.name: COVID-19 Vaccination - Sputnik V
DIA_JM_SPUTCOV_VAC.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case this vaccine brand (Sputnik V), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

DIA_JM_COVID_SCRE.name: COVID-19 Screening (Negative)
DIA_JM_COVID_SCRE.description: The patient requests to have a COVID-19 test/screening since they either have been in close contact with an infected person and/or having symptoms of COVID-19.

DIA_JM_COVID_P_SCRE.name: COVID-19 Screening (Positive)
DIA_JM_COVID_P_SCRE.description: The patient requests to have a COVID-19 test/screening since they either have been in close contact with an infected person and/or having symptoms of COVID-19.

SYM_CRP_ANAPHYLACTIC_SHOCK.name: Anaphylactic Shock
SYM_CRP_ANAPHYLACTIC_SHOCK.description: Anaphylactic shock is a rare but severe allergic reaction that can be deadly if you don't treat it right away. It's most often caused by an allergy to food, insect bites, or certain medications. A shot of a drug called epinephrine is needed immediately.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART.name: Healthy Heart
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART.description: The patient has a healthy heart shape and function.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LUNGS.name: Healthy Lungs
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LUNGS.description: The patient has a pair of healthy lungs.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_GASTROINTESTINAL_TRACT.name: Healthy Gastrointestinal Tract
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_GASTROINTESTINAL_TRACT.description: The patient has a healthy GI tract.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_KIDNEYS.name: Healthy Renal System
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_KIDNEYS.description: The patient has a healthy renal system.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SPLEEN.name: Healthy Spleen Function
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SPLEEN.description: The patient has a healthy spleen.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NERVES.name: Healthy Nerves
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NERVES.description: The patient has healthy nerves with healthy sensations.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_PANCREAS.name: Healthy Pancreas
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_PANCREAS.description: The patient has a healthy pancreas with healthy insulin levels.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_TEETH.name: Healthy Dental Health
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_TEETH.description: The patient has healthy dental health.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_REFLEX.name: Healthy Reflex
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_REFLEX.description: The patient has a healthy reflex when the doctor performs a physical exam.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART_RHYTHM.name: Normal Heart Rhythm
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_HEART_RHYTHM.description: The patient has a normal heart rhythm when the patient's heart is attached to a ECG or being heard by using a stethescope.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BREATHING.name: Normal Breathing Patterns
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BREATHING.description: The patient has a normal breathing pattern.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_PRESSURE.name: Normal Blood Pressure
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_PRESSURE.description: The patient has a normal blood pressure which is between 90/60mmHg and 120/80mmHg.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NEUROLOGICAL_FUNCTION.name: Healthy Neurological Function
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_NEUROLOGICAL_FUNCTION.description: The patient has a healthy neurological function.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_EYESIGHT.name: Normal Eyesight
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_EYESIGHT.description: The patient has a normal eyesight.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CELL_COUNT.name: Healthy Blood Cell Count
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CELL_COUNT.description: The patient has a healthy blood cell count.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CONTENT.name: Healthy Amount of Proteins and Glucose
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_BLOOD_CONTENT.description: The patient has a healthy amount of Proteins and Glucose in their blood.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_VITAMIN_LEVELS.name: Healthy Vitamin Levels
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_VITAMIN_LEVELS.description: The patient has a healthy amount of vitamins in his/her blood.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SMELL.name: Healthy Smell and Taste
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_SMELL.description: The patient has a healthy sense of smell and taste.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_STOOLS.name: Healthy Stools
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_STOOLS.description: The patient has healthy stool shape and colour.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_URINE.name: Healthy Urine
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_URINE.description: The patient has healthy urine colour and urine content.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LIFESTYLE.name: Healthy Lifestyle
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_LIFESTYLE.description: The patient is exercising and having a balanced schedule.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_DIET.name: Healthy Diet
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_DIET.description: The patient is having healthy and balanced meals.

SYM_JM_HEALTHY_WEIGHT_AND_HEIGHT.name: Normal Body Mass Index
SYM_JM_HEALTHY_WEIGHT_AND_HEIGHT.description: The patient has a healthy Body Mass Index. Body Mass Index (BMI) is a person’s weight in kilograms divided by the square of height in meters. A high BMI can be an indicator of high body fatness. BMI can be used to screen for weight categories that may lead to health problems but it is not diagnostic of the body fatness or health of an individual.

SYM_JM_COVID_SCREEN_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Screening Appointment
SYM_JM_COVID_SCREEN_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with their GP to get a COVID PCR Swab Test.

SYM_JM_SYMPTOMS.name: COVID-19 Symptoms
SYM_JM_SYMPTOMS.description: The patient is experiencing symptoms of COVID-19, a immediate PCR or RT-PCR test must be performed to diagnose the disease.

SYM_JM_ASTHMA.name: Asthma
SYM_JM_ASTHMA.description: The patient has a history or currently has Asthma and they require a inhaler.

SYM_JM_AUTOIMMUNE_DISEASE.name: Autoimmune Disease
SYM_JM_AUTOIMMUNE_DISEASE.description: The patient has Autoimmune Disease like Lupus or Vasculitis and require medications.

SYM_JM_DIABETES.name: Diabetes
SYM_JM_DIABETES.description: The patient has a history of Diabetes or currently has Diabetes and they require insulin injections and diet changes.

SYM_JM_DRUG_ABUSE.name: Drug Abuse
SYM_JM_DRUG_ABUSE.description: The patient has a history of drug abuse or currently has a drug addiction and they require counseling and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_GERD.name: Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease
SYM_JM_GERD.description: The patient has a history or currently has Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease and they require Antacids and Diet Changes.

SYM_JM_GINGIVITIS.name: Gingivitis
SYM_JM_GINGIVITIS.description: The patient has currently has Gingivitis which is the inflammation of the gums, or gingiva. It commonly occurs because a film of plaque, or bacteria, accumulates on the teeth. Gingivitis is a non-destructive type of periodontal disease, but untreated gingivitis can progress to periodontitis. This is more serious and can eventually lead to loss of teethand the patient needs special toothpaste and medications.

SYM_JM_HIV.name: HIV
SYM_JM_HIV.description: The patient has currently has HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) which may lead to AIDS and they require antiretroviral therapy.

SYM_JM_OVERWEIGHT.name: Overweight/Obese
SYM_JM_OVERWEIGHT.description: The patient has a history or currently is overweight/has obesity and they require diet and lifetyle changes.

SYM_JM_SMOKING.name: Smoking
SYM_JM_SMOKING.description: The patient has a history or currently has a smoking addiction and they require counseling and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_MALNUTRITION.name: Malnutrition
SYM_JM_MALNUTRITION.description: The patient is currently malnourished and requires immediate care with a food pump.

SYM_JM_MENTAL_HEALTH_ISSUES.name: Mental Health Issues
SYM_JM_MENTAL_HEALTH_ISSUES.description: The patient has a history or currently is suffering from Depression or Anxiety and required counseling.

SYM_JM_SEDITARY_LIFESTYLE.name: Sedentary Lifestyle
SYM_JM_SEDITARY_LIFESTYLE.description: The patient has a sedentary lifestyle which can cause health problems and they need lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_HIGH_CHOLESTEROL.name: High Cholesterol
SYM_JM_HIGH_CHOLESTEROL.description: The patient has high cholesterol which increases the patients risk of stroke and heart attack.

SYM_JM_HYPERTENSION.name: Hypertension
SYM_JM_HYPERTENSION.description: The patient has a blood pressure higher than 120/80mmHg, which increases the risk of stroke and hert attack and they require hypertensive medications.

SYM_JM_ALPHA_VARIANT.name: Previous Infection of Alpha Variant
SYM_JM_ALPHA_VARIANT.description: The patient has previously been infected with the Alpha (B.1.1.7) Variant of COVID-19, which was first detected in the UK. This is a Variant of Concern (VOC).

SYM_JM_BETA_VARIANT.name: Previous Infection of Beta Variant
SYM_JM_BETA_VARIANT.description: The patient has previously been infected with the Beta (B.1.351) Variant of COVID-19, which is from South Africa. This is a Variant of Concern (VOC)

SYM_JM_DELTA_VARIANT.name: Previous Infection of Delta Variant
SYM_JM_DELTA_VARIANT.description: The patient has previously been infected with the Delta (B.1.617.2) Variant of COVID-19, which was first detected in the India. This is a Variant of Concern (VOC) and also a double mutant (meaning this variant has 2 main mutations).

SYM_JM_GAMMA_VARIANT.name: Previous Infection of Gamma Variant
SYM_JM_GAMMA_VARIANT.description: The patient has previously been infected with the Gamma (P.1) Variant of COVID-19, which was first detected in Brazil. This is a Variant of Concern (VOC).

SYM_JM_ARRHYTHMIA.name: Arrhythmia
SYM_JM_ARRHYTHMIA.description: An arrhythmia is a problem with the rate or rhythm of the heartbeat. During an arrhythmia, the heart can beat too fast, too slowly, or with an irregular rhythm. When a heart beats too fast, the condition is called tachycardia. When a heart beats too slowly, the condition is called bradycardia. The patient requires antiarrhythmics.

SYM_JM_CARDITIS.name: Myocarditis
SYM_JM_CARDITIS.description: Myocarditis, also known as inflammatory cardiomyopathy, is inflammation of the heart muscle. Symptoms can include shortness of breath, chest pain, decreased ability to exercise, and an irregular heartbeat. The duration of problems can vary from hours to months. Complications may include heart failure due to dilated cardiomyopathy or cardiac arrest.

SYM_JM_VALVE_DISEASE.name: Valvular Heart Disease
SYM_JM_VALVE_DISEASE.description: Valvular heart disease is when any valve in the heart has damage or is diseased. There are several causes of valve disease. The normal heart has four chambers (right and left atria, and right and left ventricles) and four valves (Figure 1). Diseases include Stenosis and Regurgitation.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_HEART_DISEASE.name: Severe Heart Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_HEART_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of heart disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from a heart attack, heart failure, severe coronary heart disease, severe angina and etc.

SYM_JM_CARDIAC_INFECTION.name: Endocarditis
SYM_JM_CARDIAC_INFECTION.description: Endocarditis is an infection that affects the inner membrane that separates the chambers and valves of the heart (endocardium). Heart infection symptoms can include: Fever, Shortness of breath, Weakness or fatigue, Swelling in your legs or abdomen, Changes in your heart rhythm, Dry or persistent cough, Skin rashes or unusual spots.

SYM_JM_CARDIOMYOPATHY.name: Cardiomyopathy
SYM_JM_CARDIOMYOPATHY.description: Cardiomyopathy (kahr-dee-o-my-OP-uh-thee) is a disease of the heart muscle that makes it harder for your heart to pump blood to the rest of your body. Cardiomyopathy can lead to heart failure. The main types of cardiomyopathy include dilated, hypertrophic and restrictive cardiomyopathy. Treatment — which might include medications, surgically implanted devices, heart surgery or, in severe cases, a heart transplant — depends on which type of cardiomyopathy you have and how serious it is.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_LUNG_DISEASE.name: Severe Lung Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_LUNG_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of lung disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from ARDS, Pulmonary Embolism, End-stage Lung Cancer, Severe Pneumonia and etc.

SYM_JM_PNEUMONITIS.name: Pneumonitis
SYM_JM_PNEUMONITIS.description: Pneumonitis (noo-moe-NIE-tis) is a general term that refers to inflammation of lung tissue. Technically, pneumonia is a type of pneumonitis because the infection causes inflammation. Pneumonitis, however, is usually used by doctors to refer to noninfectious causes of lung inflammation.

SYM_JM_LUNG_INFECTION.name: Pneumonia
SYM_JM_LUNG_INFECTION.description: Pneumonia is an infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs. The air sacs may fill with fluid or pus (purulent material), causing cough with phlegm or pus, fever, chills, and difficulty breathing. A variety of organisms, including bacteria, viruses and fungi, can cause pneumonia.

SYM_JM_PULMONARY_FIBROSIS.name: Pulmonary Fibrosis
SYM_JM_PULMONARY_FIBROSIS.description: Pulmonary fibrosis is a lung disease that occurs when lung tissue becomes damaged and scarred. This thickened, stiff tissue makes it more difficult for your lungs to work properly. As pulmonary fibrosis worsens, you become progressively more short of breath. The scarring associated with pulmonary fibrosis can be caused by a multitude of factors.

SYM_JM_DIVERTICULITIS.name: Diverticulitis
SYM_JM_DIVERTICULITIS.description: Diverticula are small, bulging pouches that can form in the lining of your digestive system. They are found most often in the lower part of the large intestine (colon). Diverticula are common, especially after age 40, and seldom cause problems. The presence of diverticula is known as diverticulosis (die-vur-tik-yoo-LOE-sis). When one or more of the pouches become inflamed, and in some cases infected, that condition is known as diverticulitis (die-vur-tik-yoo-LIE-tis). Diverticulitis can cause severe abdominal pain, fever, nausea and a marked change in your bowel habits.

SYM_JM_CROHNS.name: Crohn's Disease
SYM_JM_CROHNS.description: Crohn's disease is a type of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) that may affect any segment of the gastrointestinal tract from the mouth to the anus. Symptoms often include abdominal pain, diarrhea (which may be bloody if inflammation is severe), fever, abdominal distension, and weight loss.

SYM_JM_ULC.name: Ulcerative Colitis
SYM_JM_ULC.description: Ulcerative colitis (UC) is an inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). It causes irritation, inflammation, and ulcers in the lining of your large intestine (also called your colon). There's no cure, and people usually have symptoms off and on for life.

SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS.name: Small Hemorrhoids
SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS.description: Hemorrhoids (HEM-uh-roids), also called piles, are swollen veins in your anus and lower rectum, similar to varicose veins. Hemorrhoids can develop inside the rectum (internal hemorrhoids) or under the skin around the anus (external hemorrhoids). Nearly three out of four adults will have hemorrhoids from time to time. Hemorrhoids have a number of causes, but often the cause is unknown. Fortunately, effective options are available to treat hemorrhoids. Many people get relief with home treatments and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS1.name: Small Hemorrhoids
SYM_JM_HEMORRHOIDS1.description: Hemorrhoids (HEM-uh-roids), also called piles, are swollen veins in your anus and lower rectum, similar to varicose veins. Hemorrhoids can develop inside the rectum (internal hemorrhoids) or under the skin around the anus (external hemorrhoids). Nearly three out of four adults will have hemorrhoids from time to time. Hemorrhoids have a number of causes, but often the cause is unknown. Fortunately, effective options are available to treat hemorrhoids. Many people get relief with home treatments and lifestyle changes.

SYM_JM_CELIAC_DISEASE.name: Coeliac Disease
SYM_JM_CELIAC_DISEASE.description: Celiac disease, sometimes called celiac sprue or gluten-sensitive enteropathy, is an immune reaction to eating gluten, a protein found in wheat, barley and rye. If you have celiac disease, eating gluten triggers an immune response in your small intestine. Over time, this reaction damages your small intestine's lining and prevents it from absorbing some nutrients (malabsorption). The intestinal damage often causes diarrhea, fatigue, weight loss, bloating and anemia, and can lead to serious complications. In children, malabsorption can affect growth and development, besides causing the symptoms seen in adults.

SYM_JM_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease, also called chronic kidney failure, describes the gradual loss of kidney function. Your kidneys filter wastes and excess fluids from your blood, which are then excreted in your urine. When chronic kidney disease reaches an advanced stage, dangerous levels of fluid, electrolytes and wastes can build up in your body. In the early stages of chronic kidney disease, you may have few signs or symptoms. Chronic kidney disease may not become apparent until your kidney function is significantly impaired.

SYM_JM_END_CKD.name: Chronic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_END_CKD.description: Chronic kidney disease, also called chronic kidney failure, describes the gradual loss of kidney function. Your kidneys filter wastes and excess fluids from your blood, which are then excreted in your urine. When chronic kidney disease reaches an advanced stage, dangerous levels of fluid, electrolytes and wastes can build up in your body. In the early stages of chronic kidney disease, you may have few signs or symptoms. Chronic kidney disease may not become apparent until your kidney function is significantly impaired.

SYM_JM_KIDNEY_STONES.name: Kidney Stones
SYM_JM_KIDNEY_STONES.description: Kidney stones (also called renal calculi, nephrolithiasis or urolithiasis) are hard deposits made of minerals and salts that form inside your kidneys. Diet, excess body weight, some medical conditions, and certain supplements and medications are among the many causes of kidney stones. Kidney stones can affect any part of your urinary tract — from your kidneys to your bladder. Often, stones form when the urine becomes concentrated, allowing minerals to crystallize and stick together.

SYM_JM_GLUMEROLUNEPHRITIS.name: Glumerolunephritis
SYM_JM_GLUMEROLUNEPHRITIS.description: Glomerulonephritis (gloe-mer-u-low-nuh-FRY-tis) is inflammation of the tiny filters in your kidneys (glomeruli). Glomeruli remove excess fluid, electrolytes and waste from your bloodstream and pass them into your urine. Glomerulonephritis can come on suddenly (acute) or gradually (chronic).

SYM_JM_POLYCYSTIC_KD.name: Polycystic Kidney Disease
SYM_JM_POLYCYSTIC_KD.description: Polycystic kidney disease (PKD) is an inherited disorder in which clusters of cysts develop primarily within your kidneys, causing your kidneys to enlarge and lose function over time. Cysts are noncancerous round sacs containing fluid. The cysts vary in size, and they can grow very large. Having many cysts or large cysts can damage your kidneys. Polycystic kidney disease can also cause cysts to develop in your liver and elsewhere in your body. The disease can cause serious complications, including high blood pressure and kidney failure.

SYM_JM_UTI.name: Urinary Tract Infection
SYM_JM_UTI.description: A urinary tract infection (UTI) is an infection in any part of your urinary system — your kidneys, ureters, bladder and urethra. Most infections involve the lower urinary tract — the bladder and the urethra. Women are at greater risk of developing a UTI than are men. Infection limited to your bladder can be painful and annoying. However, serious consequences can occur if a UTI spreads to your kidneys. Doctors typically treat urinary tract infections with antibiotics. But you can take steps to reduce your chances of getting a UTI in the first place.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_UT_DISEASE.name: Severe Renal Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_UT_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of renal disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from Renal Failure, Chronic Kidney Disease - End Stage and etc.

SYM_JM_PARKINSONS.name: Parkinson's Disease
SYM_JM_PARKINSONS.description: Parkinson's disease is a progressive nervous system disorder that affects movement. Symptoms start gradually, sometimes starting with a barely noticeable tremor in just one hand. Tremors are common, but the disorder also commonly causes stiffness or slowing of movement. In the early stages of Parkinson's disease, your face may show little or no expression. Your arms may not swing when you walk. Your speech may become soft or slurred. Parkinson's disease symptoms worsen as your condition progresses over time.

SYM_JM_ALZHEIMERS.name: Alzheimer's Disease
SYM_JM_ALZHEIMERS.description: Alzheimer's disease is a progressive disorder that causes brain cells to waste away (degenerate) and die. Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of dementia — a continuous decline in thinking, behavioral and social skills that disrupts a person's ability to function independently. The early signs of the disease may be forgetting recent events or conversations. As the disease progresses, a person with Alzheimer's disease will develop severe memory impairment and lose the ability to carry out everyday tasks. Current Alzheimer's disease medications may temporarily improve symptoms or slow the rate of decline. These treatments can sometimes help people with Alzheimer's disease maximize function and maintain independence for a time.

SYM_JM_DEMENTIA.name: Dementia
SYM_JM_DEMENTIA.description: Dementia describes a group of symptoms affecting memory, thinking and social abilities severely enough to interfere with your daily life. It isn't a specific disease, but several different diseases may cause dementia. Though dementia generally involves memory loss, memory loss has different causes. Having memory loss alone doesn't mean you have dementia. Alzheimer's disease is the most common cause of a progressive dementia in older adults, but there are a number of causes of dementia. Depending on the cause, some dementia symptoms may be reversible.

SYM_JM_SEVERE_NEURO_DISEASE.name: Severe Neurological Disease
SYM_JM_SEVERE_NEURO_DISEASE.description: The patient has a very serious case of neurological disease and you must treat it soon. The patient might be currently suffering from Encephalitis, Meningitis, Paralysis, Intercranial Pressure and etc.

SYM_JM_LACTOSE_INTOLERANCE.name: Lactose Intolerance
SYM_JM_LACTOSE_INTOLERANCE.description: The patient has lactose intolerance which causes bloating, diarrhea and flatulance, the patient requires diet changes.

SYM_JM_TAPEWORM_INFECTION.name: Tapeworm Infections
SYM_JM_TAPEWORM_INFECTION.description: The patient has a tapeworm in their intestines and requires anti-parasitics.

SYM_JM_SCURVY.name: Scurvy
SYM_JM_SCURVY.description: The patient has scurvy which is caused by vitamin C deficiency, which is very rare in wealthy countries. The patient requires immediate intravenous infusions.

SYM_JM_B12_DEFICIENCY.name: Vitamin B12 Deficiency
SYM_JM_B12_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a Vitamin B12 Deficiency which can cause neuro degeneration and cognitive decline if untreated.

SYM_JM_MAGNESIUM_DEFICIENCY.name: Magnesium Deficiency
SYM_JM_MAGNESIUM_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a magnesium deficiency and they require magnesium supplements.

SYM_JM_IRON_DEFICIENCY.name: Iron Deficiency
SYM_JM_IRON_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a iron deficiency, which can cause anemia and they require iron supplements.

SYM_JM_IODINE_DEFICIENCY.name: Iodine Deficiency
SYM_JM_IODINE_DEFICIENCY.description: The patient has a iodine deficiency which can cause cognitive decline and they require supplements of iodine.

SYM_JM_SEIZURES.name: Post-Surgical Seizures
SYM_JM_SEIZURES.description: Short episodes of seizures caused by an abnormal neuronal activity after surgery.

SYM_JM_UNDERLYING_ISSUES.name: Underlying Issues
SYM_JM_UNDERLYING_ISSUES.description: The patient has a history of a illness/disorder like Anxiety or Diabetes.

SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Annual Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her General Practitioner to get an annual checkup.

SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.name: Cardiovascular Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Cardiologist to get an cardiovascular checkup.

SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Neurological Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Neurologist to get an neurological checkup.

SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.name: Gastrointestinal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Gastroenterologist to get an gastroesophageal checkup.

SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.name: Renal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT2.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Nephrologist to get an renal checkup.

SYM_JM_NUTRI_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Dietary Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_NUTRI_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Nutritionist to get an dietary checkup.

SYM_JM_COVID_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Post-COVID Infection Checkup
SYM_JM_COVID_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with their General Practitioner to get a whole body examination after being affected by COVID-19.

SYM_JM_VACCINE_FOLLOWUP.name: COVID Vaccine Followup
SYM_JM_VACCINE_FOLLOWUP.description: The patient has an appointment with their General Practitioner to get a full physical exam after taking a vaccine, because they are having slight symptoms or a adverse reaction.

SYM_JM_MMR_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: MMR Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_MMR_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Mumps, Measles and Rubella Vaccination.

SYM_JM_INFLU_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Influenza Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_INFLU_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Influenza Vaccination which protects the patient from H1N1, H3N1 and 2 different types of Influenza B infections.

SYM_JM_VARICL_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Varicella Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_VARICL_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Varicella Vaccination which protects the patient from Shingles and Chickenpox.

SYM_JM_DTAP_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: DTaP Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_DTAP_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (Whooping Cough) Vaccination.

SYM_JM_PNEUMOCC_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Pneumoccocal Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_PNEUMOCC_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Pneumoccocal Vaccination.

SYM_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Anthrax Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Anthrax Vaccination.

SYM_JM_EBOLA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Ebola Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_EBOLA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Ebola Vaccination.

SYM_JM_DENGUE_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Dengue Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_DENGUE_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Dengue Vaccination.

SYM_JM_MALARIA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Malaria Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_MALARIA_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Malaria Vaccination.

SYM_JM_TB_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: Tuberculosis Vaccination Appointment
SYM_JM_TB_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Tuberculosis Vaccination.

SYM_JM_AZCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - AstraZeneca
SYM_JM_AZCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a AstraZeneca COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_SINOCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - SinoVac
SYM_JM_SINOCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a SinoVac COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_SINOPCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Sinopharm
SYM_JM_SINOPCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Sinopharm COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_PFICOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Pfizer
SYM_JM_PFICOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Pfizer COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_MODCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Moderna
SYM_JM_MODCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Moderna COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_JNJCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Johnson N Johnson
SYM_JM_JNJCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Johnson n Johnson COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_SPUTCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Sputnik V
SYM_JM_SPUTCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a Sputnik V COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_CANCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - CanSino
SYM_JM_CANCOV_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a CanSino COVID-19 Vaccine for protection from the new coronavirus; Coronavirus Disease - 2019 (COVID-19).

SYM_JM_NEGATIVE_COVID_SCREEN.name: COVID-19 - Not Detected
SYM_JM_NEGATIVE_COVID_SCREEN.description: The patient has not tested positive for SARS-COV-2 (COVID-19) after a RT-PCR or PCR was performed. It is likely to be some other disease like the Flu or Bronchitis, patient should be sent home.

SYM_JM_POSITIVE_COVID_SCREEN.name: COVID-19 - Positive
SYM_JM_POSITIVE_COVID_SCREEN.description: The patient has tested positive for SARS-COV-2 (COVID-19) after a RT-PCR or PCR was performed. The patient could be infected with the Alpha, Beta, Gamma or Delta variants and should be placed in isolation immediately.

SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Annual Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_ANNUAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her General Practitioner to get an annual checkup.

SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.name: Cardiovascular Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_CARDIOVASCULAR_CHECKUP_APPOINTMEN.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Cardiologist to get an cardiovascular checkup.

SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Neurological Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_NEUROLOGICAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Neurologist to get an neurological checkup.

SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.name: Gastrointestinal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_GASTROINTESTINAL_CHECKUP_APPOINTM.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Gastroenterologist to get an gastroesophageal checkup.

SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.name: Renal Checkup Appointment
SYM_JM_KIDNEY_CHECKUP_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her Nephrologist to get an renal checkup.

SYM_JM_COVID_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.name: COVID-19 Vaccination Appointment - Trial/Emergency Use
SYM_JM_COVID_VACCINE_APPOINTMENT.description: The patient has an appointment with his/her GP to get a COVID-19 Vaccination for trials or for emergency use authorised by the government.

EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.name: Colonoscopy
EXM_CRP_COLONOSCOPY.description: A colonoscopy is a medical procedure that is done to look for cancer, colon polyps or other abnormalities inside your bowel. Colonoscopy is done using an instrument called a colonoscope (a type of endoscope) — a long, thin, flexible tube containing a camera and a light.

EXM_JM_MEDICAL_HISTORY.name: Family and Medical History
EXM_JM_MEDICAL_HISTORY.description: Medical history: In clinical medicine, the patient's past and present which may contain relevant information bearing on their health past, present, and future. The medical history, being an account of all medical events and problems a person has experienced is an important tool in the management of the patient..

TRT_CRP_EPIPEN.name: Epinephrine Autoinjector
TRT_CRP_EPIPEN.description: An epinephrine autoinjector is a medical device for injecting a measured dose or doses of epinephrine by means of autoinjector technology. It is most often used for the treatment of anaphylaxis. \n\nRequired room: Any office

TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.name: Active Monitoring
TRT_CRP_ACTIVE_MONITOR.description: Active monitoring is when the oncologist recommends no treatment but monitors the patient's health with regular check-ups and blood tests. This is sometimes called “careful monitoring” or “watch and wait”. \n\nRequired room: Any Oncology office

TRT_JM_CHECKUP.name: Whole Body Examination
TRT_JM_CHECKUP.description: The doctor gives the patient a whole body examination or also known as a Checkup.

TRT_JM_LIFESTYLE_CHANGES.name: Lifestyle Changes
TRT_JM_LIFESTYLE_CHANGES.description: The doctor recommends that the patient changes their lifetyle to be more active and healthy.

TRT_JM_COURSE_OF_MEDICATIONS.name: A Course of Medication/Supplements
TRT_JM_COURSE_OF_MEDICATIONS.description: The doctor gives the patient a course of medication or supplements to treat existing diseases, minor problems or disorders.

TRT_JM_SPECIALIST_BOOKING.name: Specialist Booking
TRT_JM_SPECIALIST_BOOKING.description: The doctor refers the patient to a specialist to treat their disease/disorder or investigate their case.

TRT_JM_HOME.name: Send Home
TRT_JM_HOME.description: The patient has no issues, so the doctor has sent them home.

TRT_JM_ISOLATION.name: Isolation
TRT_JM_ISOLATION.description: The patient is in need of isolation at a hospital or home, since they have tested positive for a infectious disease such as COVID-19.

TRT_JM_COUNSELING.name: Counseling
TRT_JM_COUNSELING.description: Counselling is a talking therapy that involves a trained therapist listening to you and helping you find ways to deal with emotional issues. Sometimes the term "counselling" is used to refer to talking therapies in general, but counselling is also a type of therapy in its own right.

TRT_JM_INSULIN_THERAPY.name: Insulin Therapy
TRT_JM_INSULIN_THERAPY.description: Medication that lowers the level of blood sugar. \n\nRequired room: Office / Diagnostic unit at Internal Medicine dpt.

TRT_JM_MMR_VACCINATION.name: MMR Vaccination
TRT_JM_MMR_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Mumps, Measles and Rubella (MMR) vaccine will be administered prevent Mumps, Measles and Rubella.

TRT_JM_INFLU_VACCINATION.name: Influenza Vaccination
TRT_JM_INFLU_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Influenza vaccine will be adinistered prevent H1N1, H3N1 and 2 different types of Influenza B infections.

TRT_JM_VARICL_VACCINATION.name: Varicella Vaccination
TRT_JM_VARICL_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Varicella vaccine will be administered to prevent Chickenpox and Shingles.

TRT_JM_DTAP_VACCINATION.name: DTaP Vaccination
TRT_JM_DTAP_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (DTaP) vaccine will be administered to prevent Diptheria, Tetanus and Pertusis (Whooping Cough).

TRT_JM_PNEUMOCC_VACCINATION.name: Pneumoccocal Vaccination
TRT_JM_PNEUMOCC_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Pneumoccocal vaccine will be administered to prevent Pneumoccocal Meningitis, Bacteremia and Pneumonia.

TRT_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINATION.name: Anthrax Vaccination
TRT_JM_ANTHRAX_VACCINATION.description: A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular infectious disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Anthrax vaccine will be administered to prevent Anthrax.

TRT_JM_DENGUE_VACCINATION.name: Dengue Fever Vaccination

A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, a Dengue Fever vaccine will be administered to prevent Dengue Fever.

TRT_JM_EBOLA_VACCINATION.name: RVSV-ZEBOV Vaccination

A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe, its toxins, or one of its surface proteins. In this case, the rVSV-ZEBOV vaccine will be administered to prevent Ebola.

In this case, a COVID Vaccine in trial will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

In this case this vaccine brand (Sputnik V), will be administered to prevent COVID-19.

TRT_JM_SUPPORTIVE_CARE.name: Supportive Therapy
TRT_JM_SUPPORTIVE_CARE.description: Supportive therapy consists of numerous symptomatic treatments, mainly for viral diseases where no specific treatment exists.
